name: Vimuktatman
dates_notes: Pre-Vidyāraṇya by citation; dated by internal references and citation chronology.
core_thesis: Vimuktātman is a key transitional figure in the Vivaraṇa line — between Sarvajñātman / Prakāśātman upstream and Vidyāraṇya downstream — whose principal contribution is a systematic defence of the *anirvacanīya-bhāva-rūpā-avidyā* doctrine against alternative analyses of *adhyāsa* (especially *anyathā-khyāti*, *asat-khyāti*, and *ātma-khyāti*). His central claim is that *avidyā* is positively existent (*bhāva-rūpā*) yet indeterminable as either *sat* or *asat*, that it has *Brahman* alone as its *āśraya*, and that the *jīva* is a *cidābhāsa*, a reflection (*pratibimba*) of *Brahman* in this beginningless ignorance. He gives careful Advaitic refutations of the realist *khyāti*-theories of error and defends the Advaitic *anirvacanīya-khyāti* — the object of erroneous cognition is itself indeterminable, neither real nor unreal. His treatment of the *avacchedavāda* / *pratibimbavāda* alternatives helps fix the technical vocabulary that Vidyāraṇya, Madhusūdana, and later Vivaraṇa authors will use; the *Iṣṭa-Siddhi* is the standard reference work cited within Advaita on the metaphysics of *avidyā* and the analysis of *adhyāsa*.

engaged_works.title: Ishta-Siddhi
engaged_works.summary: Independent *prakaraṇa* in eight chapters whose stated purpose is to establish (*siddhi*) the *iṣṭa* — the desired tenet, namely *brahmātmaikya*. Vimuktātman's method is to take up each opposed analysis of *adhyāsa* and erroneous cognition (*ātma-khyāti* of the Vijñānavādins, *asat-khyāti* of the Mādhyamikas as Advaitically construed, *anyathā-khyāti* of the Naiyāyikas, *akhyāti* of the Prābhākaras) and show that each fails on its own terms, leaving *anirvacanīya-khyāti* as the only coherent account. He defends *bhāva-rūpā-anirvacanīya-avidyā* with *Brahman* as locus, examines the *avaccheda-vāda* and *pratibimba-vāda* analyses of the *jīva* and inclines toward the latter, and refutes Bhedābheda and the proto-realist alternatives of his day. Jñānottama's *Vivaraṇa* is its principal sub-commentary. The treatise is cited as authoritative by Vidyāraṇya in the *Vivaraṇa-Prameya-Saṃgraha* and by Madhusūdana in the *Advaita-Siddhi*, and supplies much of the technical machinery later Advaitins use to discuss *adhyāsa* and *avidyā*.
